observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of taxpayers based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office starting with active taxpayers and expanding the roll by aiming to increase it annually until all taxpayers are covered; and
(b) Municipal Accountant (i) set up the RPT/SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and provide and maintain subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer, starting with those active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies; and (ii) exert efforts to trace the unreconciled discrepancies of the four reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: The MAO and MTO continuously failed to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Accounting Office to conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to trace the discrepancy.
observations.reason: The MAO still failed to act on the observations due to the voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact to the authorized government depository banks as required under existing regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer to:
(a) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies needed using the prescribed modes of procurement as provided under Section 53.5 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011; 
(b) secure a Certificate for Non Availability of Stock from the DBM-PS or its equivalent before resorting to alternative modes of procurement; and
(c) abide by the requirements of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property.
observations.reason: The municipality still failed to procure from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management conduct a gender analysis using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool as required in Section C.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for the attainment of the objectives of the GAD Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) and the proper determination of the amounts that may be attributed to the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the management properly plan the PPAs for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as identified under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 – 1.
observations.reason: Still, PPAs charged against the fund do not partake the nature of capital investments.
The municipality failed to fully comply with the utilization, accounting, and reporting guidelines on LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly signed certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible every beginning of the year; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to recognize the RPT/SET receivables for CYs 2017 to 2021 based on the records of the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as follows:

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Treasurer (MT) prepare and submit a Schedule of Unreleased Checks and ensure that the reconciliation of records with the Municipal Accountant is regularly observed; and (b) Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the prescribed Check Disbursement Process under the NGAS specifically on the preparation of Check Disbursements Journal and Journal Entry Vouchers based on the RCI submitted by the MT and revert to the respective cash and liability accounts all unreleased, stale and cancelled checks as at year-end.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to (a) determine the causes of the discrepancies between the accounting books and the cash books for subsequent appropriate action; and (b) conduct reconciliation of cash account records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books affected.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert utmost effort to trace the discrepancies between the cash in bank per book and bank statement and the reciprocal accounts, and effect any adjustment thereafter.

We recommended that the:
LDRRM Officer (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002; and (ii) include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years as well as the funds from other sources;
Management ensure that all charges to the LDRRMF are eligible expenditures and supportive of the purpose for which the fund was intended; and
Municipal Accountant (i) comply with the correct recognition in the books of accounts of all funds received by the municipality; (ii) submit the necessary supporting documents identified in the observation; (iii) draw an entry to recognize in the books all unexpended portion of DRRMF-MOOE and DRRM-QRF of the LDRRMF every year-end; (iv) prepare a note to financial statements pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002 to inform the users of the financial statement of the status of the LDRRMF as of reporting period.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Chairman and Members of the BAC and TWG to (i) carefully scrutinize the documentary requirements submitted by the bidder and conduct verification thereof to ascertain any commitment by the bidder to arrive at a decision compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) comply with the necessary posting requirements;
Municipal Engineer to conduct close monitoring during project implementation and ensure compliance by the contractor with the requirements of the contract; and
Municipal Accountant, in close coordination with the BAC, to (i) submit all the necessary supporting documents for the identified contracts; and (ii) verify all supporting documents as to the completeness and ensure that the same formed part of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers before effecting payments thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) consider the aforecited rules and regulations on the hiring of service providers; (b) strictly implement control measures on the required submission of DTRs by service providers prior to actual day of payout as basis in the preparation of payrolls; (c) avoid hiring of service providers whose functions either overlap or duplicate with those of the regular employees or are considered recurring, necessary, and essential to the Municipality; (d) encourage the service providers to take the Civil Service Exam for them to be qualified and be filled in as regular or permanent employees of the Municipality by providing them free review and payment fee for such exam; and (e) ensure that the service providers, who have reached the compulsory retirement age of 65, are occupationally qualified based on the positions applied for or offered.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) immediately submit to the Audit Team the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers; and (b) strictly observe the timeframes on the rendition of accounts including copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and other pertinent issuances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
through the MENRO and Liga ng mga Barangay, (i) encourage the active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws; and (ii)  strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level in order to achieve a community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
as far as the Municipal budget permits, provide necessary support in enjoining barangays of the Municipality to establish their own or clustered MRF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
in his capacity as Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), consider the amendment of the composition or reconstitution of the GFPS-TWG to include all other mandatory members thereof and create the M&E Team to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of gender mainstreaming efforts of the Municipality;
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, to exert effort in establishing a GAD database which will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
instruct the GAD FPS to (i) continually comply with the GAD mechanisms and processes to sustain the LGU’s critical consciousness in supporting gender and development, women’s empowerment and responding to gender issues pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the AAO to (a) fast-track the updating of records of personnel whose details have been affected by a step increment or promotion and validate the unremitted balance of ₱1,212,107.66 to facilitate the prompt remittance of premiums to the GSIS; (b) reconcile the monthly electronic billing statement with the actual deductions from payrolls in order to take necessary actions should discrepancy exist; (c) make a representation with the GSIS to reconcile the disparity between the records of the Municipality and the GSIS; (d) act promptly on the required updating of records of personnel to avoid the recurring accumulation of unremitted premiums; and (e) ensure that remaining loan balances of any employee will be settled timely to avoid incurrence of penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office (i) remit the PhilHealth contributions of the Job Order Personnel on a monthly basis, same with the permanent employees, to avoid future issues; (ii) review, investigate, and reconcile the long outstanding balance of each account and recognize adjusting entries, if necessary; and (iii) prepare the following entry to correct the ending balance of the Due to PhilHealth account as at year-end, as follows:

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office to (a) account for the remaining balance of ₱274,235.76 and effect necessary actions; and (b) apply the new rate of percentage tax for non-VAT registered suppliers.

observations.reason: Management failed to conduct its annual PPE inventory and maintain PPE records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to:
(a) determine the causes of the discrepancies between the accounting books and the cash books for subsequent appropriate action; and 
(b) conduct reconciliation of cash account records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The MTO tried to identify possible causes of the discrepancy. On the other hand, MAO still failed to act on the observation due to the voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Petty Cash Custodian to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs particularly on the accounting for PCF under the imprest fund system.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to perform its duties relating to post-audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) immediately render those accounts already due for submission and 
(b) strictly comply with the regulations on the rendition of accounts in order not to delay the verification and audit of transactions in the appropriate period.
observations.reason: Disbursement vouchers were still not submitted completely to this office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
(a) instruct the HR department to (i) initiate the conduct of the review of the agency’s organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement; (ii) install internal control on the hiring and supervision/management of all personnel; and (iii) refer to the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 for the policy guidelines for contract of services; and
(b) require the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to strengthen internal control over the (i) release of the payment of salaries and wages; and (ii) processing of payroll and liquidation reports by checking the sufficiency of the supporting documents for each and every type of transaction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) through the MENRO and Liga ng mga Barangay, strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level in order to achieve community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and (b) through the MENRO, General Services Office and Municipal Health Office address the current issues concerning the health safety of SWM personnel and the Materials Recovery Facility to effectively operate for safe and efficient sorting, processing, storage and recycling of waste materials.
observations.managementAction: Uniforms, gloves and masks were provided by the Municipality to the SWM personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that for future programs with similar nature, the concerned personnel evaluate thoroughly the identified beneficiaries by requiring them to provide all the necessary information, particularly their family members, occupation and income as basis in the assessment of their eligibility to the program.

Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWD Officer and barangay officials concerned to submit a comprehensive report on the issues in the implementation and distribution of SAP.

We recommended that management strictly enforce settlement by persons liable for the audit disallowances within the prescribed period, pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommend that management send demand letters to the last known address of inactive persons liable.
observations.reason: The audit disallowance was partially settled as of December 31, 2021 leaving unsettled balance of ₱325,602.37.
Officials failed to reconcile their records
Municipality set up its RPT/SET receivable similar to the amount recorded from the previous years
Discrepancies on reciprocal accounts
All commonly-use office supplies were purchased from third party
Copies of POs/Contracts were not submitted
Prescribed time for rendition of accounts not observed

Job order contracts are continuously and automatically being renewed every six (6) months and their compensation was pegged on a monthly basis instead of per day basis












Non referral to DTR on payroll
Projects charged against the fund did not qualify as capital investment
Lack of training and power tools
Failure to effectively implement Solid Waste Management Policies
Excess funds from some fund transfers were not yet returned
Disallowance of ₱335,302.37 remained unsettled as of December 31, 2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office, Treasurer’s Office and Inventory Committee, as the case may be, to (a) conduct complete physical count of all properties and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; (b) prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for each group of PPE; (c) keep property cards for each class off PPE; (d) set up a spreadsheet application or lapsing schedule for the PPE items to facilitate the computation for the periodic depreciation for each PPE item; and (e) facilitate confirmation with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and seek technical assistance, if necessary, for the titling of all lots in the name of the Municipality of Masantol.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to (a) determine the causes of the discrepancies between the accounting books and the cash books for subsequent appropriate action; and (b) conduct reconciliation of cash account records at least quarterly to identify the nature of differences and record the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Petty Cash Custodian to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs particularly on the accounting for PCF under the imprest fund system.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Treasurer prepare the certified list of taxpayers based on the Assessment Roll from the Municipal Assessor’s Office starting with active taxpayers and expanding the roll by aiming to increase it annually until all taxpayers are covered; and
Municipal Accountant (i) set up the RPT/SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and provide and maintain subsidiary ledgers of individual taxpayer, starting with those active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies; and (ii) exert efforts to trace the unreconciled discrepancies of the four reciprocal accounts.
We recommended that management instruct the Accounting Office to conduct regular reconciliation of reciprocal accounts to trace the discrepancy.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact to the authorized government depository banks as required under existing regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer to (a) procure, as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies needed using the prescribed modes of procurement as provided under Section 53.5 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011; (b) secure a Certificate for Non Availability of Stock from the DBM-PS or its equivalent before resorting to alternative modes of procurement; and (c) abide by the requirements of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 to enable the Audit Team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) immediately render those accounts already due for submission and (b) strictly comply with the regulations on the rendition of accounts in order not to delay the verification and audit of transactions in the appropriate period.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive
instruct the HR department to (i) initiate the conduct of the review of the agency’s organizational structure and staffing to determine the appropriate manpower complement; (ii) install internal control on the hiring and supervision/management of all personnel; and (iii) refer to the provisions of CSC Resolution No. 02-0790 for the policy guidelines for contract of services; and
require the Accounting and Treasurer’s Office to strengthen internal control over the (i) release of the payment of salaries and wages; and (ii) processing of payroll and liquidation reports by checking the sufficiency of the supporting documents for each and every type of transactions.

We recommended that Management conduct a gender analysis using the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool as required in Section C.4 of the PCW-DILG-DBM NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for the attainment of the objectives of the GAD Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) and the proper determination of the amounts that may be attributed to the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the management properly plan the PPAs for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund and refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of capital investments or those not essential or relevant to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as identified under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 – 1.

We recommended that the:
MDRRMO and members of the emergency response team (i) attend trainings to gain knowledge and understanding, awareness and skills for prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery; and (ii) maintain the minimum standards of power tools/heavy equipment at the minimum as required by the disaster preparedness minimum standards volume 2 of the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council;
management (i) explain the deficiencies noted on the procurement of Elf Truck; (ii) make sure that specifications for the procurement of goods shall be based on relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements and that reference to brand names shall not be allowed; (iii) submit the documentary deficiencies for the transactions stated above; and (iv) observe the existing rules and regulations on procurements; and
Municipal Accountant and the Acting GSO install an effective internal control in the recording, management and issuance of supplies and materials in accordance with the above-mentioned guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
through the MENRO and Liga ng mga Barangay, strengthen the implementation of existing ordinances and policies on solid waste management at the barangay level in order to achieve community compliance and eventually abide by the provisions on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
through the MENRO, General Services Office and Municipal Health Office address the current issues concerning the health safety of SWM personnel  and the Materials Recovery Facility to effectively operate for safe and efficient sorting, processing, storage and recycling of waste materials.

We recommended that the management (a) explain the deviations made on the prescribed rules and guidelines for the BGCM under LBC No. 125 and strictly comply with the requirements provided for all grant and funds received (b) submit the documentary deficiencies for the transactions stated above and henceforth observe the existing guidelines on emergency procurements including the preparation and submission of documents for auditing purposes; (c) ensure that all procurement during the year are within the approved Annual Procurement Plan and in accordance with Republic Act 9184; and (d) return the unutilized fund to the National Treasury.

We recommended that for future program with similar nature, the concerned personnel evaluate thoroughly the identified beneficiaries by requiring them to provide all the necessary information, particularly their family members, occupation and income as basis in the assessment of their eligibility to the program.
Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWD Officer and barangay officials concerned to submit a comprehensive report on the issues in the implementation and distribution of SAP.

We recommended that management strictly enforce settlement by persons liable for the audit disallowances within the prescribed period, pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommend that management send demand letters to the last known address of inactive persons liable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
observations.reason: The Municipality continually failed to effectively implement Solid Waste Management Policies

observations.recommendation: provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining 17 barangays to establish their own MRF.

(a) Local Chief Executive and heads of concerned implementing offices implement without further delay those remaining projects not completed/ implemented in CY 2018 and to continue to monitor and supervise the effective and efficient implementation of projects funded from various source agencies within the timelines of the MOA, as far as feasible and
(b) Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to be prompt in the return of excess funds to the source agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal HRMO: require the physical presence of personnel and the submission of their duly verified/accepted Daily Time Records (DTRs) bi-monthly and monthly Accomplishment Report before payment of their salaries;
observations.reason: Inconsistent and unreliable information on the Daily Time Records and Payroll Sheet were observed.

observations.recommendation: prepare the Statement of Duties and Responsibilities detailing the work assignment and functions of every hired JO to prevent duplication of tasks between them and the regular/permanent employees;
evaluate timely the performance of personnel and review the necessity of their employment; and

maintain individual personnel file of pertinent records and documents at the HRM Office upon hiring such as PDS, resume, government issued photo-bearing ID, job order contract and the statement of duties and responsibilities for each job.

observations.managementAction: Actual physical count of the properties of the Municipality is presently undergoing. All offices were already directed to submit the present inventory of properties under their custody while the Office of the Treasurer and Office of the Accountant were already directed to submit records of all properties for review and comparison with the actual physical count. A supply officer will be appointed to manage all properties and records thereof. The concerned offices are likewise instructed to update and maintain all records pertaining to the properties of the Municipality following the form and procedure prescribed by laws, rules and regulations. So far, the properties located in Sto. Nino and Sta. Lucia were already issued title. On the other hand, the issuance of title for properties where the PNP station and the Covered Basketball Court are located is underway.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting General Services Officer, improve its compliance with regard to acquisition of commonly-used supplies directly from the PS-DBM and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Even prior to the receipt of the AOM, a meeting was held wherein all concerned officials were advised to fully comply with the procurement process prescribed by R.A. No. 9184, its IRR and all other issuances pertaining to procurement.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in AAR 2018
The LGU continue to purchase commonly-used supplies from the outside suppliers instead from the PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the Audit Team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.
observations.managementAction: Masantol has no General Services Officer at present on account of its fiscal limitation. Thus, the Office of the Treasurer had assumed most of the duties and functions of the GSO. Unfortunately,  due to lack of personnel,  volume of records pertaining to contracts/purchase orders, as well as the other equally demanding tasks of the Office of the Treasurer, it was not able to fully comply within the period prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. Nonetheless, the concerned official and employees will be directed to henceforth exert all efforts to fully comply with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. If necessary, the present personnel at the Office of the Municipal Treasurer will be augmented for this purpose.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in AAR 2018
Management failed to comply with the submission of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and AIR to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated and recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert more efforts to improve compliance with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and post-audit activities.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices were directed to exert all efforts, and maximize available resources to improve compliance with Section 347, R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated 15 September 2009.
observations.reason:
Reiterated in AAR 2018
The Agency failed to submit its accounts within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, thru the LCPC, comply with the provisions set by Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) 2012-120 on the direction setting, budget allocation and utilization of the fund for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities (PPAs).
observations.managementAction: All concerned officials will be directed to fully comply with Section 15, R.A. No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) 2012-120 on the direction setting, budget allocation and utilization of fund for LCPC.

Executive Order No. 5 dated 07 March 2017 was issued reorganizing the Municipal Incident Command System (MICS) structure, composition and defining its team function as an on-scene disaster response and management mechanism under the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management System. On the other hand, full completion of the composition of the MDRRMC in accordance with R.A. No. 10121 is hindered by the fact that no member of the AFP is assigned in Masantol. Conversely, the MDRRMO will be directed to ensure that the MICS is fully functional and that all concerned personnel/responders be given proper training.  While the existing 5year DRRM Plan covers 2019, the MDRRMO will be directed to prepare the 10 year DRRM Plan for approval. Finally, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will be tasked to install an effective internal control in the recording, maintenance, and distribution of relief items and disaster response equipment.  Auditor’s Rejoinder: During the interview, we were told by the LDRRM Officer that the Municipality has no organized ICS for the CY 2017. Furthermore, management has not submitted a copy of the said Executive Order to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members pursue its plan of conducting a review and evaluation of the latest Local Revenue Code and consider, as far as practicable, the revision of the same to conform to the requirements of RA No. 7160 on the revision of Local Code every five years.  Otherwise, continue finding ways and means to increase its collection efficiency to generate more revenues.
observations.managementAction: Prior to the receipt of the AOM, a meeting between the Office of the Mayor, the Municipal Treasurer, and Chairman of the Committee on Ways and Means of the Sangguniang Bayan was already held to discuss the proposed Revision of the Masantol Revenue Code. The proposal was formally transmitted to the Sangguniang Bayan last May 2018 for its appropriate action.
observations.reason: Management failed to update its Local Revenue Code as of December 31, 2018.

observations.managementAction: For 2018, the SSS and Philhealth contributions of Job Order workers are being remitted by the Municipality in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular 2014-83.  On the other hand, the HR and the Municipal Accountant will be directed to fully comply with Section 77, R.A. No. 7160, Section 2, Rule XI, Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 40, Section 4, P.D. No. 1445.

The LSB and concerned officials of the Municipality were advised to fully observe the provisions of DepEd-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2017 on the allocation and utilization of the SEF. Further, positive steps were taken to recover the P3,938.00 overpayment from the supplier. Relative hereto, the Office of the Municipal Accountant was enjoined to carefully check the completeness/correctness of the amount to be paid before payment of all transaction by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office, Treasurer’s Office and Inventory Committee, as the case may be, to (a) conduct complete physical count of all properties and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; (b) prepare and maintain property cards  for each category of assets starting with current year’s acquisition; (c) facilitate confirmation with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and seek technical assistance, if necessary, to be able to have the remaining 11 lots titled in the name of the Municipality of Masantol.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer to (a) procure the common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM as provided under Section 53.5 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011; (b) secure a Certificate for Non Availability of Stock from the DBM-PS or its equivalent before resorting to alternative modes of procurement; and (c) abide by the requirements of the New Government Accounting System on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the Audit Team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) address with urgency the manpower and competency issues faced by the Municipal Accounting Office; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the regulations on the rendition of accounts in order not to delay the verification and audit of transactions in the appropriate period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining 17 barangays to establish their own MRF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to invest in high-impact capital expenditure projects in the utilization of its 20% Development Fund and refrain from proposing non-capital investment projects/programs under the Fund.

We recommended that the:
Members of the MDRRMC institute the update of functional organization of the ICS, the required training and resource management which include the personnel, teams, equipment, and other facilities available during emergencies, as a perfect tool in responding to all types of hazards most particularly in an on-scene emergency situations and calamities such as flooding; and
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer install an effective internal control in the recording, maintenance and distribution of relief items and disaster response equipment in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive and heads of concerned implementing offices  implement without further delay those remaining projects not completed/ implemented in CY 2018 and  to continue to monitor and supervise the effective and efficient implementation of projects funded from various source agencies within the timelines of the MOA, as far as feasible and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to be prompt in the return of excess funds to the source agencies upon completion or termination of the project.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce settlement by persons liable for the audit disallowances within the prescribed period, pursuant to the 2009 Revised Rules of Procedures of the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommended that Management send demand letters to the last known address of inactive persons liable.

observations.managementAction: As of 2018, all cash advances granted were duly and fully liquidated. Further, even before receipt of the AOM, all concerned officials of the Municipal Government were directed to fully comply with the laws, rules, and regulations governing the grant and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, to cause the establishment of GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation provided by Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government has Rapid Community Based Monitoring System (RCBMS) which is now a part of the overall management information system (MIS) of the LGU. The MPDO is in charge of the RCBMS but other offices, including the MSWD, the GAD Focal Person, and the TWG may avail of the data contained therein which will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

 through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and

as far as the Municipal budget permits, provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the remaining 20 barangays to establish their own MRF.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO informed the 26 punong barangays of Masantol in a meeting called for such purpose regarding the solid waste management program of Masantol.  They were further reminded about R.A. No. 9003 as well as the ordinances enacted by the Sangguniang Barangay concerning solid waste management.  The establishment of barangay MRF was likewise highlighted as well as the segregation at source policy. For 2018, there are 13 barangays already with MRF but more efforts will be undertaken to enjoin the remaining barangays to fully comply with Section 32 of RA No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) require the Municipal Development Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan;
 
(b) stop the practice of charging to the 20% Development Fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/ capital expenditures; and 

(c) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council (MDC) will be convened and advised to specifically describe the projects included in the Annual Investment Plan. Further, the MDC will be reminded about the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017 of the DILG and DBM, particularly Item 3 thereof. Finally, the BAC and the Municipal Engineer will be instructed to properly plan and schedule procurement activities relative to the development projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the project implementers:

(a) implement and complete without further delay all the remaining projects not yet finished as of year-end;

(b) adhere to the Memorandum of Agreement agreed upon by both parties (Funding agencies and the implementing agency) and pertinent project implementation guidelines for the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects; and

(c)  improve procurement planning and closely monitor and supervise the projects to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that management ensure the proper implementation of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowances which have become final and executory in accordance with the approved settlement schedule.
observations.reason: Reiterated in AAR 2018

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office, Treasurer Office and Inventory Committee, as the case may be to:

 (a) conduct complete physical count of all properties and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment;

(b) strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory results; and

(c) facilitate confirmation with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and seek technical assistance, if necessary, to be able to have the lots titled in the name of the Municipality of Masantol.
observations.managementAction: Currently in the process of facilitating the titles to be named to the Municipality

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:

to submit immediately to the Audit Team all accounts and financial reports due for submission; and

henceforth strictly comply with the regulation and due dates on the rendition of accounts and financial reports.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload and lack of accounting personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and reiterated that the Local Chief Executive

enjoin all employees to refrain from procuring capital expenditure items and other similar items thru cash; and

require the Municipal Treasurer to pay all purchases through checks except for petty expenditures to avoid the possible misuse of government funds and to be able to comply with the BIR regulations pursuant to Section 93 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Some of the transactions were immediate in nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members conduct a review and evaluation of the latest Local Revenue Code and consider, as far as practicable, the revision of the same to conform to the requirements of RA No. 7160 on the revision of Local Code every five years.  Otherwise, continue finding ways and means to increase its collection efficiency to generate more revenues.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Office, Treasurer Office and Inventory Committee, as the case may be (a) to conduct complete physical count of all properties and submit the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment; (b) prepare and maintain property cards  for each category of assets starting with current year’s acquisition; (c) facilitate confirmation with the Land Registration Authority (LRA) and seek technical assistance, if necessary, to be able to have the remaining 11 lots titled in the name of the Municipality of Masantol.

We recommended that the management (a) discontinue the grant of cash advances to elected officials other than for travelling expenses; (b) prohibit the issuance of additional cash advances to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made; (c) grant cash advance only to those permanently appointed and bonded officials; and (d) improve compliance to the laws, rules and regulations in the granting and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Acting General Services Officer, improve its compliance with regard to acquisition of commonly-used supplies directly from the PS-DBM and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned improve compliance in furnishing the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, together with the GAD Focal Person and TWG, to cause the establishment of GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation provided by Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive
through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and coordinate efforts in the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
as far as the Municipal budget permits, provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the remaining 20 barangays to establish their own MRF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Development Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan; (b) stop the practice of charging to the 20% Development Fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/ capital expenditures; and (c) instruct the BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.

We recommended that the Management, thru the LCPC, comply with the provisions set by Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular (MC) 2012-120 on the direction setting, budget allocation and utilization of the fund for the strengthening and implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities (PPAs).

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members pursue its plan of conducting a review and evaluation of the latest Local Revenue Code and consider, as far as practicable, the revision of the same to conform to the requirements of RA No. 7160 on the revision of Local Code every five years.  Otherwise, continue finding ways and means to increase its collection efficiency to generate more revenues.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

 (a) the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact to the authorized government depository banks as required under existing regulations; and

(b) all accountable officers to maintain and keep updated the cashbooks on a daily basis.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal HRMO to:

 (a) require the physical presence, as long as feasible, of personnel during payroll release dates and encourage the prompt claiming of salaries.

(b) prepare the Statement of Duties and Responsibilities detailing the work assignment and functions of every hired JO to prevent duplication of tasks between them and regular/permanent employees;

(c) require the submission of their duly verified/accepted accomplishment report every month before payment of their salaries; and

(d) evaluate timely the performance of personnel and review the necessity of their     employment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

 (a) direct the procurement section to (i) prioritize acquisition of commonly-used supplies through PS-DBM before resorting to the other alternative mode or shopping;  and (ii) in case of non-availability of stocks, secure a Certificate/s of Non Availability of Stocks from the DBM-PS or its equivalent as a supporting document to the disbursement vouchers; and

(b) require the Municipal Accountant to abide by the requirements of the New Government Accounting System on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property.

observations.reason: Barangays are still in the process of establishing their own MRF

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MSWD Officer:

(a) exert best efforts for the immediate and full implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program;

(b) closely coordinate with the partner agency (DSWD) to fast track the release of fund and cycle menu upon signing of the Memorandum of Agreement and for the smooth implementation of the program and

(c) require the  focal person for all Day Care Centers to ensure that all procurements are supported by Official Receipts, whenever practicable.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(a) instruct the MDC to set program/project/activity for the amount of ₱63,871.80 under 20% Development Fund;

(b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries; and

(c) see to it that all projects to be funded out of the 20% development fund shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that the:

Local Chief Executive (i) as head of the MDC, ensure the completion of the composition of the MDRRMC in accordance with the RA 10121.

Members of the MDRRMC (i) institute the functional organization of the ICS, the required training and resource management which include the personnel, teams, equipment, and other facilities available during emergencies, as a perfect tool in responding to all types of hazards most particularly in an on-scene emergency situations and calamities such as flooding; (ii) ensure that the member CSOs/NGOs to the Council possess the eligibility requirements cited under NDRRMC No.  3 to assure of effective and strengthened citizen’s participation in the locality;

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (i) facilitate the transfer of unexpended balance of the LDRMF to the Trust Fund accounts; and (ii) install an effective internal control in the recording, maintenance and distribution of relief items and disaster response equipment in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal officials continue to comply with the provisions of the LGC on the allowable disbursements pertaining to leagues and federation of local elective officials.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that management ensure the proper implementation of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowances which have become final and executory in accordance with the approved settlement schedule.
observations.reason: The Municipality has enforced the full settlement of disallowed PEI  and partial settlement of disallowed COLA

(b) secure a Certificate for Non Availability of Stock from the DBM-PS or its equivalent before resorting to alternative modes of procurement; and

(c) abide by the requirements of the New Government Accounting System on the purchase, recognition and issuances of Supplies and Property.

 (a) prohibit the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursement unless the item/service is petty in nature; and

(b) comply strictly with the guidelines and principles of an imprest system wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier for internal control purposes and so that the applicable taxes may be deducted from the purchases.

require the physical presence, as long as feasible, of personnel during payroll release dates and encourage the prompt claiming of salaries.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Inspection and Acceptance Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the audit team to perform its duties relating to post audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) to submit immediately to the Audit Team all accounts and financial reports due for submission; and (b) henceforth strictly comply with the regulation and due dates on the rendition of accounts and financial reports.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) institutionalize a working and functional GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) or a similar GAD mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Province; (b) encourage and direct the GFPS Committee and Technical Working Group to perform its mandated tasks; (c) cause the establishment of GAD Database; and (d) require the GAD focal person to prepare its accomplishment report based on the approved GPB.

Otherwise, continue finding ways and means to increase its collection efficiency to generate more revenues.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that management ensure the proper implementation of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and enforce the immediate settlement of the disallowances which have become final and executory in accordance with the approved settlement schedule.
